SHUBHANG SHARMA.
$\sum_i\varphi_i$
~ MAY 03.2026, HYDERABAD.

ACADEMIC
MATH-PH
FIELDNOTES
BLOG
CREATIVE

The beauty of the triviality of mathematics

I have been noticing an unsettling feeling that I often have after learning a new mathematical or physical subject. Scholarly speaking, any such endaevour is quite an enjoyable process on its own. But ever since I did my master’s thesis, something has been bugging me. The fact that I couldn’t end my project on a more practical note, ideally steering towards a more concrete computation or perhaps even making attempts at a novel calcilation. There are three things I need unwrap before I describe the unsettling feeling post-new-subject-learning. That should provde a good context - an abstract base to then tie the ends easily.

As much as I (one) can downplay my works and experience, there are solid reasons to believe I did do a good job. Skip to ACT III - I did a good job for that part. It’s the tricky waters of intersecction of two fields where multiple ideas and approaches make the transition slighlty intricate. To figure out those transition maps, that too smoothly is indeed non-trivial. But I don’t think the smoothness of an academic career is expected by default. Science can be disruptive, non-linear and of course challenging. One should communicate their best efforts during the process, definitely not hide the fun along the process. They just shouldn’t hide behind it.

ACT I - My master’s thesis

Firstly, I ofcourse did it without any interaction with a mathematician. That mattered primarily in the sense that, I was not aware of exprectations of a mathematician from a project. I was definitely guided by my advisor about a possible practical end to the thesis, and I was of course aiming towards that. Secondly, the huge intrinsic learning curve/barrier to mathematics research slowed down things quite a bit. Of course, one can also wonder, what should the mindset of an undergradiate or a master’s student be at the later ends of their education (especially in these intersection waters). In my case, I was still lagging in terms of basic math education. Yet, I also had a decent exposure to deep ideas and advanced gaduate elements like differential geometry (not until the last semester though). So naturally navigating these interdisciplinary waters of math and physics where one of the subjects has a huge enetry barrier while math-ph was an alien of a field locally was admittedly tricky. Whatever it is, it’s an irrefutable guilty feeling about the direction of my thesis.

So, ever since the master’s thesis ended, I have this huge sense of practical outcomes of scholarship. Reading is a good way to understand and get into matehamtics. But how long am I going to do that? How long am I going to delay my entry into math. Will my actions lead to a position.

ACT - II - I did do a good job

Let me narrate my thesis direction here. The primary goal was eventually to tackle a reasonably novel theoretical computation using the tools of AdS/CFT correspondence. So my first semester on this project included learning these tools. I focused on geometry of the Anti-de Sitter space, and Conformal Field theory. During the second semester, I read about the correspondence and established key links between the two main objects. It was an interestingly challenging exercise to navigate physics literature. The loose (pragmatic in their own ways) arguments and everything. I am proud of it acctually, to have navigated through that and settle on a good level of udnerstanding appreciating the nuacnes and getting ready for the next steps. So, it was time to tackle a computation. My advisor had an idea to compute. He sent me a paper that is directly relevant to the computation, wihcih provides the motivation to cahange a few things and extract some physics. It is during this point I finally come to peace with my inclination towards more mathematical research than research based on vague arguments. Of course the vaieness is relative. These are more vague to a mathematician, these are only highly pragmatic and rich for physicists.It’s natural that I felt this way because all ym undergrad years, I had a inclination towards mathematical rigor and setup over physcial language.

Upon discussing these issues with my advisor, we thought it would be good to connect to the literature of mathematics in these areas.

First was the compactness issues of Einstein manifolds related to the confromal boundary and AdS geometry.

Second was about mathematical conformal field theory.

Both these works involved primarily a solid language of Riemannian geometry. So it was only natural for me to advacnce my differential geometry knowledge further and discuss AdS/CFT in that language.

So I started doing that, I read Riemannian Geometry. I think it’s a real deal. It took the rest of the semetster to do that.

While I was learning Riemannian geometry, I also focused on how AdS/CFT interacts with mathematics. I learned about conformal geometry, conformal invariants, Fefferman Graham’s formalism about construction of these invariants. So in this sense, I was heading towards presenting AdS/CFT as setup for computing conformal invariants. This was really exciting because, there was a global decomposition of conformal invariants predicted by physicists. Which was highly non-trivial mathematically and was only proved completely by a student of Feffarman in 2012.

There is a lot of intersection between math and physics around manifold invariants. Gauge theory is a very solid example of this. So, my backgground here is bery strong in this sense. I am going to expand my thesis a little to include a potential calculation about these conformal invariants. Conformal anomalies are again at the root of this discussion. One can actually cpompute these anomalies using AdS/CFT. So can we do that nicely? Something I am going to do in discussion with my advisor soon.

And on the other hand I have this project in gauge theory and knot invariants! These are indeed very pracitcally conenccetd you see! seem strong.

Anyway, my current empty feeling is gauge theoretic stuff… Just because I am not yet able to do research! Atleeast get a position. And especially because I am reading all of this on my own. So yeah I need to connect with mathematicisa sand get a research position!

Good good. Lots of confidence and power to me!!! Can probably target physicsts interested in matehamtics too, especially in conversations with my advisor! In math I can defly talk to my manifolds instructor, where I developed good lecture notes on Differential geoemtry. ALl these are in their rough forms a bit and the goals of June and July is to indeed make this presentable!!! Let’s go.

And the other aspects is the explaining the break time and provving basicc mathematics. That I need to do with my scholarly values an blogs and expositiory/pedagogical articles, along with potentially exams! GRE and all that!

Possible Shubhang Possible! I can indeed make this work!!! Let’s go!!!

Of course all this should come together in the SOP!!! And very strong recommendation letters!!!

So prioirities:

Thesis + GT-LDT + DG Notes + Blogs + Articles. Hmmmm Let’s go. I will look into thesis today!!

Tomorrow I will continnue the GT thing, that should in principle finish my “new” stuff endaevor. I need to finsih it this month!!! Yeah!! Let’s go!!!

After today’s stint in Conformal Invariants, I go back to GT LDT.

From July I start with blogs again! Also thesis stuff and DG notes stuff ofcourse! Yeah… gave me a good boost now this blog.

Art $\times$ Science
